---
title: "Using the ExPanD app"
---

The `ExPanD` app wraps the analytical functions in an interactive, no-code interface (built
with [Shiny for Python](https://shiny.posit.co/py/)). It is provided by the `app` extra:

```bash
pip install "expdpy[app]"
```

## Launching on a DataFrame

```python
from expdpy.app import ExPanD
from expdpy.data import load_russell_3000, load_russell_3000_data_def

ExPanD(
    load_russell_3000(),
    df_def=load_russell_3000_data_def(),   # identifies the panel dimensions
)
```

This opens a browser with a sidebar (sample, subset filter, outlier treatment, save/load
config, notebook export) and a stack of analysis cards: descriptive statistics, histogram,
extreme observations, correlations, time trends, scatter plot, by-group views and a
regression builder.

## Cross-sectional data

If you do not provide a time-series identifier, the time-trend components are dropped:

```python
ExPanD(load_gapminder())  # treated as cross-sectional
```

## Starting from an upload dialog

Call `ExPanD()` with no data to start with an in-app file upload (CSV/Excel/parquet):

```python
ExPanD()
```

## Reproducible export

The **Export notebook + data** button downloads a zip containing the prepared analysis
sample (parquet) plus a Jupyter notebook and a `.py` script that recreate every displayed
component with `expdpy` calls — so an analysis done in the app can be reproduced in code.

## Saving configurations

**Save config** downloads the current analysis configuration as JSON (optionally encrypted
when `store_encrypted=True`); **Load config** restores it. You can also pass a configuration
at launch:

```python
from expdpy.data import load_worldbank, load_worldbank_data_def, load_worldbank_var_def, get_config

ExPanD(
    load_worldbank(),
    df_def=load_worldbank_data_def(),
    var_def=load_worldbank_var_def(),
    config_list=get_config("worldbank"),
)
```
